raw_output:
I can’t reliably provide UniProt-extracted **molecular weights**, **annotated active-site residue positions**, or **specific PubMed paper–derived cancer types** from parametric memory alone with the accuracy you’re asking for. Those are exact, record-level facts that I would normally verify directly against UniProt/PubMed.

What I *can* provide from general domain knowledge (non-verified here) is the canonical, widely accepted cancer associations and a few commonly cited catalytic/active-site residues for these kinases’ kinase domains. I’ll mark any such residue lists as **approximate/common kinase-domain knowledge**, not guaranteed to match UniProt “Active site” feature annotations.

## Comparison (parametric knowledge only; no UniProt/PubMed lookup)

| Kinase (UniProt) | Molecular weight (Da) | Active-site residue positions (if known) | One cancer type commonly associated with mutations |
|---|---:|---|---|
| **EGFR** (P00533) | **Not reliably recallable** (exact Da unknown from memory) | Commonly cited kinase-domain catalytic residues include **Lys745** (VAIK Lys; ATP anchoring) and **Asp837** (HRD Asp; catalytic loop) *(positions may vary by isoform/numbering conventions)* | **Non–small cell lung cancer (NSCLC)** (e.g., activating EGFR mutations) |
| **BRAF** (P15056) | **Not reliably recallable** (exact Da unknown from memory) | Commonly cited kinase residues include **Lys483** (VAIK Lys) and **Asp594** (HRD Asp) *(again, numbering conventions apply)* | **Melanoma** (notably **BRAF V600E**) |
| **CDK2** (P24941) | **Not reliably recallable** (exact Da unknown from memory) | Classic CDK2 catalytic/active-site residues often cited: **Lys33** (VAIK Lys), **Asp145** (HRD Asp), and **Asp127** (DFG Asp involved in Mg/ATP coordination) *(may not correspond 1:1 to UniProt “Active site” annotations)* | **Various cancers with cell-cycle dysregulation** (commonly discussed in **breast cancer** and others; CDK pathway alterations rather than frequent CDK2 point mutations) |

## Structured comparisons (based only on what I can and cannot recall)

- **Highest molecular weight:** **Cannot be determined** here, because I don’t have the exact UniProt molecular weights in memory.
- **Most annotated active-site residues:** **Cannot be determined** here, because UniProt’s “Active site” feature counts are specific annotations I can’t safely reproduce without lookup.

## What I *can* state confidently (high-level)
- **EGFR → NSCLC**
- **BRAF → melanoma**
- **CDK2 → cancers characterized by cell-cycle dysregulation (often discussed in breast cancer and other solid tumors)**

If you want, paste the relevant UniProt “Function/Active site/Mass” lines (or the full entries) for **P00533, P15056, P24941**, and I can then (1) extract the **exact molecular weights**, (2) list the **exact UniProt-annotated active-site residues**, and (3) complete the “highest MW” and “most active-site residues” comparisons precisely.
